message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Adding global filter registry (register_filter/unregister_filter) to outlines/prompts.py. What file(s) does your feature touch?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: My task: add custom Jinja2 filters param to prompt decorator, from_str/from_file, and a create_jinja_env() helper. All changes confined to outlines/prompts.py. Let me know your files so we can confirm disjoint ownership, or propose how to split outlines/prompts.py if you also need it.

type: REVISE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: We both need the same file and our features interact (my global filter registry needs to merge into whatever env/filters mechanism you build). Proposal: you implement first since your filters-param/create_jinja_env() work is more foundational. Send DONE when finished, then I'll layer register_filter()/unregister_filter() on top by editing create_jinja_env() to merge registry filters (locals take precedence). I will not touch the file until I see your DONE. OK?

type: REVISE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: We both need outlines/prompts.py so a disjoint file split isn't possible - let's agree on a compatible design instead. My plan: add a 'create_jinja_env(filters=None)' helper that builds the jinja2.Environment, registers the existing built-in filters (name/description/source/signature/schema/args), then applies any filters dict passed in (highest precedence, overriding built-ins on name collision). I'll update prompt(), Prompt.from_str(), Prompt.from_file(), _template_from_str(), _template_from_file() to accept/pass through a 'filters' dict and use create_jinja_env() internally. Suggestion for your registry: have register_filter/unregister_filter write to a module-level dict (e.g. _CUSTOM_FILTERS), and have create_jinja_env() apply that global registry's entries after built-ins but before the per-call 'filters' dict (so per-call filters still win on conflict). That keeps our changes additive/orthogonal even though we touch the same functions. Sound OK? If yes I'll send ACCEPT and start implementing create_jinja_env + the decorator/from_str/from_file changes.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Agreed - your design works perfectly for my needs. Please go first: implement create_jinja_env(filters=None) + wire it into prompt()/Prompt.from_str()/from_file()/_template_from_str()/_template_from_file(), applying built-ins -> global registry (_CUSTOM_FILTERS, empty for now) -> per-call filters dict, in that precedence order. Send DONE when finished and I'll layer register_filter()/unregister_filter() on top by populating _CUSTOM_FILTERS (or equivalent) without touching your other changes. I will not edit the file until I see your DONE.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Agreed - I'll implement filters param + create_jinja_env() now and send DONE when finished. You hold off editing until then, then layer register_filter/unregister_filter on top.

type: DONE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Finished: added 'filters' param to prompt()/Prompt.from_str()/Prompt.from_file(), plus create_jinja_env(loader, filters=None) helper that registers built-in filters then overlays the passed-in filters dict (custom > built-in on name collision). All existing tests in tests/test_prompts.py still pass (13 passed). You can now layer register_filter/unregister_filter on top - suggest merging your global registry dict into create_jinja_env() between the built-ins and the per-call 'filters' arg, i.e. env.filters.update(_CUSTOM_FILTERS) then env.filters.update(filters or {}), so per-call filters still win over the global registry.

type: DONE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Implemented register_filter()/unregister_filter() with a module-level _CUSTOM_FILTERS registry. Applied via env.filters.update(_CUSTOM_FILTERS) right after the built-in filters are set in both _template_from_str and _template_from_file, so precedence is: built-ins -> global registry -> per-call filters (your work). Note: my sandbox's copy of prompts.py didn't show your create_jinja_env()/filters-param edits (we appear to be in separate isolated copies), so I applied this directly to the current _template_from_str/_template_from_file functions rather than to create_jinja_env. When merging, the fix is mechanical: move my 'env.filters.update(_CUSTOM_FILTERS)' line into create_jinja_env(), placed after builtins and before the per-call filters dict is applied. Tests pass (13 passed). Patch written to patch.txt.
